title: Contact - Mary Glindon MP - Mary Glindon
meta_description: Member of Parliament for Newcastle Upon Tyne East and Wallsend
text_excerpt: Contact - Mary Glindon MP - Mary Glindon Skip to main content Mary Glindon Member of Parliament for Newcastle Upon Tyne East and Wallsend About Privacy Policy Contact Policy My work in Parliament More Menu Contact Mary’s role is to represent you as a constituent within the Newcastle East and Wallsend Constituency, regardless of your politics. Not sure if you’re a constituent? Check here . If you’re concerned about a local or national issue Mary can make representations on your behalf to the government, Newcastle Council or North Tyneside Council or other agencies and will keep you informed about your case. If you would like Mary’s help, please get in touch either by phone, email or post and make sure you provide the following information; Your full name and postal address A contact telephone number Any reference numbers that relate to your case A summary of your problem, including any steps you may have already taken to try and resolve it Advice Surgeries My office is open for surgeries by appointment. Please use the contact details below to make an appointment. My office is also available by email and on the telephone. Contact Mary Email – [email protected] Telephone – 0191 2342493 Write to – Mary Glindon MP, Swans Centre for Innovation, Station Road, Wallsend, NE28 6HJ MPs can help you with matters such as: Tax problems involving HM Revenue & Customs (but not issues with, for example, the council tax, which is administered by your local authority). Problems with hospitals and the NHS. Problems with benefits, pensions and national insurance. Issues dealt with by the Government departments. MPs can raise concerns with the police on behalf of a constituent, but have no role in decision-making or law enforcement. MPs do not have jurisdiction over local council decisions or policy. However, they can write to a local council and ask them to investigate a problem or reconsider an issue. MPs cannot help you Private disputes with other individuals or with companies. MPs cannot offer legal advice and have no jurisdiction over the police or the courts. Constituents are therefore advised to consult with a solicitor should they need professional advice for legal disputes. They also cannot provide any financial or business advice. An MP is not able to assist in settling family arguments or private disputes with neighbours or employers. Follow on social media Mary Glindon Further details Promoted by Eddie Darke on behalf of Mary Glindon, both at Ruby Court, 1a Wesley Way, Newcastle, NE12 9UP You can view my privacy policy here https://maryglindon.com/privacy-policy/ ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
